response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r2c5 is in acute survival mode within a cluster-wide deficit deadlock. With only 6.94 kWh remaining (18.8% SoC) and a 1.0 kW load, the household has approximately 6.9 hours of autonomy—critically short. All three neighbors (r1c5, r2c4, r3c5) are simultaneously requesting energy while rejecting outbound supply, citing their own preservation needs and systemic deadlock. The repeated rejection pattern from r2c4 explicitly warns that any outbound supply accelerates cluster-wide collapse. No trust circles exist, and no inbound supply has materialized despite repeated requests. The only viable path forward is aggressive load-shedding to 0.5 kW (reducing consumption to ~2.4 hours of autonomy runway), strict conservation of remaining SoC, and a final urgent broadcast for external grid reconnection or emergency supply. Sharing any energy now would be self-defeating; survival requires holding reserves until either grid restoration occurs or an external surplus peer emerges.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.50
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Cluster-wide deficit deadlock confirmed. All neighbors critical and non-reciprocal. Load-shedding to ≤0.5 kW mandatory. Zero outbound supply until inbound materializes and SoC recovers above 0.50. Grid reconnection or external supply required within 6.9 hours to avoid complete depletion. Broadcast urgent inbound requests to all peers and grid operator."
ttl_ticks: 42
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
